themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to social services, housing, and employment. This theme is evident in Spain, Italy, France, and Poland, where parties like Vox, Fratelli d'Italia, Lega, Rassemblement National, and Prawo i Sprawiedliwość are either implementing or advocating for such measures, often clashing with opposition and civil society groups.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Criticized for advocating 'national priority' in social aid, healthcare, and housing, often sparking debates with opposition parties and local governments.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Promoting policies that prioritize Italian citizens in public services, such as housing and social benefits, while facing opposition from left-wing parties.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Advocating for policies that prioritize French citizens in welfare and employment, often clashing with mainstream parties over immigration and integration.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Implementing policies that prioritize Polish citizens in social welfare programs, often sparking controversy over perceived exclusionary practices.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's policies and rhetoric continue to shape right-wing and far-right discourse globally, with extensive coverage of his administration's actions in the U.S. influencing narratives in the UK, Spain, France, and Italy. Key topics include immigration, military engagements, and economic policies, which are frequently cited or mirrored by right-wing parties abroad.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's policies on immigration, military engagements (e.g., Iran), and economic measures (e.g., offshore wind projects) are central to domestic and international right-wing discourse.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Frequently references Trump's immigration policies and rhetoric, aligning with his 'America First' approach in UK political debates.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Draws parallels between Trump's immigration policies and their own proposals, often citing his administration as a model for nationalist governance.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Aligns with Trump's stance on immigration and national sovereignty, often referencing his policies in French political debates.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Cites Trump's economic and immigration policies as inspiration for Italy's right-wing agenda, particularly in discussions about national sovereignty and EU relations.

themes.title: Hungary’s political shift under new leadership
themes.blurb: Hungary's recent election has led to a significant political shift, with Péter Magyar's Tisza Party taking power after 16 years of Viktor Orbán's rule. This transition is closely watched by right-wing and far-right parties in Poland, Germany, and the U.S., particularly regarding Hungary's future relationship with the EU, its stance on Ukraine, and internal reforms to address corruption and civil liberties.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Magyar's victory marks a shift in Hungary's political landscape, with promises to restore EU relations, address corruption, and reform civil liberties.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Closely monitors Hungary's political transition, particularly regarding EU relations and anti-corruption measures, as a potential model or cautionary tale.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Views Hungary's shift as a potential opportunity to strengthen ties with Budapest, particularly on issues like EU skepticism and migration.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Interested in Hungary's new leadership as a potential ally in promoting nationalist policies and challenging EU norms.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric and narratives are increasingly linked to incidents of political violence and intimidation across multiple countries. This theme is evident in Italy, Spain, the UK, and the U.S., where far-right groups and individuals have been involved in clashes, protests, and targeted attacks, often justified by inflammatory rhetoric against immigrants, political opponents, and journalists.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Far-right rhetoric has been linked to violent incidents, including clashes with protesters and attacks on political opponents.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Faces accusations of inciting violence through inflammatory rhetoric, particularly against immigrants and left-wing groups, leading to protests and counter-protests.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Far-right rhetoric has been tied to incidents of intimidation and violence, particularly during protests and political campaigns.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's rhetoric has been linked to political violence, including the recent assassination attempt and clashes at protests.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Far-right parties in Germany, France, Sweden, the UK, and Norway are experiencing a surge in polling numbers, reflecting growing public support for nationalist and anti-immigration policies. This trend is reshaping the political landscape in these countries, with far-right parties either leading in polls or gaining significant ground ahead of upcoming elections.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Leads in German polls, capitalizing on public dissatisfaction with immigration and economic policies, and positioning itself as a major political force.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Leads French polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election, with Jordan Bardella emerging as a key figure in the party's resurgence.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Gains traction in Swedish polls, influencing the political agenda on immigration and law-and-order issues.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Surges in UK polls, challenging the Conservative Party and reshaping the political landscape ahead of local and general elections.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Leads Norwegian polls, capitalizing on public dissatisfaction with immigration and economic policies, and positioning itself as a potential kingmaker in future governments.
